title: Dédalo tool register.json (v7 authoring format)
description: Hand-authorable registration file for a Dédalo tool. Place it as register.json in the tool root directory. Legacy v6 matrix-dump files (with top-level 'components'/'relations') are still accepted and converted automatically; new tools must use this format. Runtime validation mirror: tools_register::validate_register().

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for IDE validation, e.g. ../tool_common/register.schema.json

name.description: Unique tool name. Must exactly match the tool directory and class name. Convention: tool_{org}_{feature}, snake_case, ASCII only.

label.description: Display label keyed by Dédalo lang code (e.g. lg-eng). One language is enough; the client falls back across languages.

description.description: Free description keyed by lang code. HTML allowed.

dedalo_version_min.description: Minimum compatible Dédalo version. Registration is refused on older installs.

affected_models.description: Component/section model names the tool applies to, e.g. ['section','component_input_text']. Use ['all_components'] to match every component. Names are resolved against the models section (dd1342) at registration.

affected_tipos.description: Optional restriction to specific ontology tipos (e.g. ['rsc36']). Empty = no restriction.

show_in_inspector.description: Show the tool button in the section inspector panel

show_in_component.description: Show the tool button inline on matching components

require_translatable.description: Only offer the tool on translatable components

always_active.description: Tool is available to every user regardless of tools-profile authorization

labels.description: UI label strings, retrievable in JS via get_tool_label(name). One entry per (name, lang).

ontology.description: Optional tool ontology extension nodes. Tipos are renumerated to unique tool* ids at registration.

config.description: Runtime configuration definition. Properties flagged with "client": true are exposed to the browser; everything else stays server-side.

default_config.description: Factory default configuration. Overridable per install in the Tools configuration section (dd996).
